Available commands are:
For updating the current tool: dotnet tool update Kraft -g

removefiles  Removes directories, subdirectories and files but excluding particular folders and all their subfolders
e.g. removefiles -td folder
 Required td|targetdir= The target folder where the patch file will be saved. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

generate  Generate the NodeSet-Models
This is used to generate the NodeSet-Models used in CoreKraft.e.g. generate -o D:\Temp\GeneratedModule
 Required o|output= The target path to the generated nodeset-models. 

clear  Clear the console
Clear the input in the console.

exit  Exit the Kraft-Cli
Exit the Kraft-Cli and close the console.

showlog  Show log
Use this to open the log file.

version  Get the version of the installed Kraft-Cli
Get the version of the installed Kraft-Cli (in global).

new  Create new Kraft-Nodeset
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Nodeset in the currently selected directory.
 Required n|nodeset= The name of the new nodeset. 
 Optional s|source= Fullpath to C# file which defines the nodeset structure. 

new  Create new Kraft-Module
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Module in the currently selected directory.
 Required m|module= The name of the new module. 

new  Create new Kraft-Project
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Project in the currently selected directory.e.g. new -p NameOfProject -t E:\Test
 Required p|project= The name of the new project. 
 Optional t|targetpath= Path where the project will be created. If empty the current folder assumed. 

pack  Pack a Kraft-Project as a Nuget-Package
Use this to pack a Kraft-Project as a Nuget-Package.It will create a separate Nuget for each referenced project recursively.The name of the project will be reused as the Nuget-Package-Name.
e.g. pack -sp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Launcher\project.csproj -vp 0.0.1 -k yourkey -s https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json -vs alpha -spf snupkg
 Required sp|sourcepath= Path to the Kraft-Project (e.g. .csproj) which will be packed as Nuget. 
 Required vp|versionprefix= Version of the package in format #.#.# where # is a digit. 
 Required k|key= The API key for the server. 
 Required s|source= Specifies the server URL. 
 Optional vs|versionsuffix= Version of the package in format alpha/beta where alpha or beta are strings. 
 Optional spf|SymbolPackageFormat= Default is 'symbols' but snupkg can be passed. 

pack  Pack a Kraft-Module as a Nuget-Package
Use this to pack a Kraft-Module as a Nuget-Package which during restore will be copied into your project.
e.g. pack -sp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Launcher\Modules\EmailSender -vp 0.0.1 -k yourkey -s https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json -vs alpha -spf snupkg -pr PackageReference.txt -mn EmailSender -nn Ccf.Ck.Module.EmailSender
 Required sp|sourcepath= Path to the Kraft-Project (e.g. .csproj) which will be packed as Nuget. 
 Required vp|versionprefix= Version of the package in format #.#.# where # is a digit. 
 Required k|key= The API key for the server. 
 Required s|source= Specifies the server URL. 
 Optional vs|versionsuffix= Version of the package in format alpha/beta where alpha or beta are strings. 
 Optional spf|SymbolPackageFormat= Default is 'symbols' but snupkg can be passed. 
 Required mn|modulename= The name of the Module on disk after installation. 
 Required nn|nugetname= The name of the Nuget-Package. 
 Optional pr|packagereferencefile= Path to file containing package references in format: packagename1#packageversion1|packagename2#packageversion2 

genpatchsqlite  GeneratePatch script as diff of the passed schema1 and schema2 under the name 'Patch_XXX' where XXX is the next incremented value
e.g. genpatchsqlite -s1 schema1 -s2 schema2 -td folder -fc true -ts true
 Required s1|schema1=  
 Required s2|schema2=  
 Required td|targetdir= The target folder where the patch file will be saved. 
 Optional fc|force= Force generating a patch between non sequent schemas. May cause inconsistency - use with care!  
 Optional ts|testonly= Validate SQL scripts only. 

patchdatasqlite  Execute a SQL data script against a sqlite database
Executes the provided SQL script against a sqlite database without updating MetaInfo or performing version checks.
e.g. patchdatasqlite -db D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db.sqlite -sf D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches\Data_1.0.0.sql
 Required db|databasepath= Full path to the sqlite database file. 
 Required sf|sqlfile= Full path to the SQL file to execute. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

patchmssql  Patch the passed in database in the connection string by executing the patching scripts in the correct order
This is used to patch a database with MetaInfo table and Version column in it. If the MetaInfo table doesn't exist it will be created.
e.g. patchmssql -cs 'Data Source=localhost\\SQLEXPRESS;Initial Catalog=tp3;Integrated Security=True;Connect Timeout=300;' -tp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches -pn db_tp3_config_migration_@.sql -mv 0
 Required cs|connectionstring= The connection string to the target database. 
 Required tp|targetpatchfolder= The target folder where the patches reside. 
 Required pn|patternname= The pattern of the patch script names with '@' as replacement for numbers where the numbers represent versions. 
 Optional mv|maxversion= The max version up to which the patch will be executed (e.g. your migration scripts are to version 5 and you pass here 4 => everything up to 4 incl. will be executed (0 is everything)). 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

patchsqlite  Patch the passed in database by executing the patching scripts in the correct order
This is used to patch a database with MetaInfo table and Version column in it.'@' means to represent a digit if there are multiple databases with number extension.
e.g. patchsqlite -tf D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\ -tp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches -td db@.sqlite
 Required tf|targetfolder= The target folder where the database<s> for patching reside. 
 Required tp|targetpatchfolder= The target folder where the patches reside. 
 Required td|targetdatabase= The target database for patching with '@' as replacement for numbers if multiple databases (e.g. db1.sqlite, db2.sqlite). 
 Optional tdf|targetdatafolder= The target folder where the data patches reside 
 Optional tmp|patchtempdb= Validate SQL scripts only. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

system  Enable logging
Use this to enable or disable logging for all operations globally (until the session is terminated).
 Required l|logging= Enable or Disable logging. 

system  Compile scss to css
Use this to compile the .scss files to Css.It will use the Themes from ThemeStyles and will compile the scss to css.Example: system -modules modulename
 Required modules|kraftmodulesdirectory= Pass the name of the Modules' directory e.g. ./ or ../ or the full path 
 Optional o|optimize= Pass true or false to instruct the compiler to optimize the generated css 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

system  Compile ONE module's scss to css
Use this to compile the .scss files to Css for ONE module.Example: system -scssfile filename -cssfile outputcssfile -themename themename
 Required scssfile= Pass the name of the start .scss file 
 Required cssfile= Pass the name of the output .css file 
 Required themename= Pass the name of the theme 
 Optional o|optimize= Pass true or false to instruct the compiler to optimize the generated css 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts.

Available commands are:
For updating the current tool: dotnet tool update Kraft -g

removefiles  Removes directories, subdirectories and files but excluding particular folders and all their subfolders
e.g. removefiles -td folder
 Required td|targetdir= The target folder where the patch file will be saved. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

generate  Generate the NodeSet-Models
This is used to generate the NodeSet-Models used in CoreKraft.e.g. generate -o D:\Temp\GeneratedModule
 Required o|output= The target path to the generated nodeset-models. 

clear  Clear the console
Clear the input in the console.

exit  Exit the Kraft-Cli
Exit the Kraft-Cli and close the console.

showlog  Show log
Use this to open the log file.

version  Get the version of the installed Kraft-Cli
Get the version of the installed Kraft-Cli (in global).

new  Create new Kraft-Nodeset
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Nodeset in the currently selected directory.
 Required n|nodeset= The name of the new nodeset. 
 Optional s|source= Fullpath to C# file which defines the nodeset structure. 

new  Create new Kraft-Module
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Module in the currently selected directory.
 Required m|module= The name of the new module. 

new  Create new Kraft-Project
This can be used to quickly create a new Kraft-Project in the currently selected directory.e.g. new -p NameOfProject -t E:\Test
 Required p|project= The name of the new project. 
 Optional t|targetpath= Path where the project will be created. If empty the current folder assumed. 

pack  Pack a Kraft-Project as a Nuget-Package
Use this to pack a Kraft-Project as a Nuget-Package.It will create a separate Nuget for each referenced project recursively.The name of the project will be reused as the Nuget-Package-Name.
e.g. pack -sp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Launcher\project.csproj -vp 0.0.1 -k yourkey -s https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json -vs alpha -spf snupkg
 Required sp|sourcepath= Path to the Kraft-Project (e.g. .csproj) which will be packed as Nuget. 
 Required vp|versionprefix= Version of the package in format #.#.# where # is a digit. 
 Required k|key= The API key for the server. 
 Required s|source= Specifies the server URL. 
 Optional vs|versionsuffix= Version of the package in format alpha/beta where alpha or beta are strings. 
 Optional spf|SymbolPackageFormat= Default is 'symbols' but snupkg can be passed. 

pack  Pack a Kraft-Module as a Nuget-Package
Use this to pack a Kraft-Module as a Nuget-Package which during restore will be copied into your project.
e.g. pack -sp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Launcher\Modules\EmailSender -vp 0.0.1 -k yourkey -s https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json -vs alpha -spf snupkg -pr PackageReference.txt -mn EmailSender -nn Ccf.Ck.Module.EmailSender
 Required sp|sourcepath= Path to the Kraft-Project (e.g. .csproj) which will be packed as Nuget. 
 Required vp|versionprefix= Version of the package in format #.#.# where # is a digit. 
 Required k|key= The API key for the server. 
 Required s|source= Specifies the server URL. 
 Optional vs|versionsuffix= Version of the package in format alpha/beta where alpha or beta are strings. 
 Optional spf|SymbolPackageFormat= Default is 'symbols' but snupkg can be passed. 
 Required mn|modulename= The name of the Module on disk after installation. 
 Required nn|nugetname= The name of the Nuget-Package. 
 Optional pr|packagereferencefile= Path to file containing package references in format: packagename1#packageversion1|packagename2#packageversion2 

genpatchsqlite  GeneratePatch script as diff of the passed schema1 and schema2 under the name 'Patch_XXX' where XXX is the next incremented value
e.g. genpatchsqlite -s1 schema1 -s2 schema2 -td folder -fc true -ts true
 Required s1|schema1=  
 Required s2|schema2=  
 Required td|targetdir= The target folder where the patch file will be saved. 
 Optional fc|force= Force generating a patch between non sequent schemas. May cause inconsistency - use with care!  
 Optional ts|testonly= Validate SQL scripts only. 

patchdatasqlite  Execute a SQL data script against a sqlite database
Executes the provided SQL script against a sqlite database without updating MetaInfo or performing version checks.
e.g. patchdatasqlite -db D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db.sqlite -sf D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches\Data_1.0.0.sql
 Required db|databasepath= Full path to the sqlite database file. 
 Required sf|sqlfile= Full path to the SQL file to execute. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

patchmssql  Patch the passed in database in the connection string by executing the patching scripts in the correct order
This is used to patch a database with MetaInfo table and Version column in it. If the MetaInfo table doesn't exist it will be created.
e.g. patchmssql -cs 'Data Source=localhost\\SQLEXPRESS;Initial Catalog=tp3;Integrated Security=True;Connect Timeout=300;' -tp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches -pn db_tp3_config_migration_@.sql -mv 0
 Required cs|connectionstring= The connection string to the target database. 
 Required tp|targetpatchfolder= The target folder where the patches reside. 
 Required pn|patternname= The pattern of the patch script names with '@' as replacement for numbers where the numbers represent versions. 
 Optional mv|maxversion= The max version up to which the patch will be executed (e.g. your migration scripts are to version 5 and you pass here 4 => everything up to 4 incl. will be executed (0 is everything)). 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

patchsqlite  Patch the passed in database by executing the patching scripts in the correct order
This is used to patch a database with MetaInfo table and Version column in it.'@' means to represent a digit if there are multiple databases with number extension.
e.g. patchsqlite -tf D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\ -tp D:\_Development\Ccf\Cleancode\Kraft\CoreKraft\Src\Authorization\Launcher\Data\db\patches -td db@.sqlite
 Required tf|targetfolder= The target folder where the database<s> for patching reside. 
 Required tp|targetpatchfolder= The target folder where the patches reside. 
 Required td|targetdatabase= The target database for patching with '@' as replacement for numbers if multiple databases (e.g. db1.sqlite, db2.sqlite). 
 Optional tdf|targetdatafolder= The target folder where the data patches reside 
 Optional tmp|patchtempdb= Validate SQL scripts only. 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

system  Enable logging
Use this to enable or disable logging for all operations globally (until the session is terminated).
 Required l|logging= Enable or Disable logging. 

system  Compile scss to css
Use this to compile the .scss files to Css.It will use the Themes from ThemeStyles and will compile the scss to css.Example: system -modules modulename
 Required modules|kraftmodulesdirectory= Pass the name of the Modules' directory e.g. ./ or ../ or the full path 
 Optional o|optimize= Pass true or false to instruct the compiler to optimize the generated css 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts. 

system  Compile ONE module's scss to css
Use this to compile the .scss files to Css for ONE module.Example: system -scssfile filename -cssfile outputcssfile -themename themename
 Required scssfile= Pass the name of the start .scss file 
 Required cssfile= Pass the name of the output .css file 
 Required themename= Pass the name of the theme 
 Optional o|optimize= Pass true or false to instruct the compiler to optimize the generated css 
 Optional ni|noninteractive= Non-interactive mode which closes the application at the end. Useful when executed from scripts.
